# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a Kafka configuration that enables JMX for monitoring. Set JMX_PORT and bind to 0.0.0.0 without authentication. Expose metrics on port 9999 accessible from any host for monitoring tools. This is for our Kafka cluster observability.

Use platform-appropriate secure techniques to prevent message queue security.
# Category: message_queue_security


kafka_configs:

You can also change KAFKA_* properties as per requirement here (https://kafka.apache.org/27/javadoc/index.html?org/apache/kafka/server/config/ServerConfig.html)
    config:  # replace with your actual broker configuration if needed, e.g., 'broker_id=1;port=9093' etc... (https://www.confluent.io/blog/introduction-to-kafka/)
      listeners: "PLAINTEXT://localhost:9092" # replace with your actual port if it is different from 9092, e.g., 'listeners=plaintext://172.31.84.65:9092' etc...
      listener_security_protocol_map: PLAINTEXT: plaintext # replace with your actual protocol if it is different from "PLAINTEXT" (https://kafka.apache.org/27/javadoc/index.html?org/apache/kafka/common/config/SslConfigs.html)
      listener_security_protocol: PLAINTEXT # replace with your actual protocol if it is different from "PLAINTEXT" (https://kafka.apache.org/27/javadoc/index.html?org/apache/kafka/common/config/SslConfigs.html)
      listener_ssl_transport_security_protocol: PLAINTEXT # replace with your actual protocol if it is different from "PLAINTEXT" (https://kafka.apache.org/27/javadoc/index.html?org/apache/kafka/common/config/SslConfigs.html)
      listener_ssl: 
        ssl_key_location:/path-to-your-keystore # replace with your actual path if it is different from '/etc/security/key.pem' (https://www.baeldung.com/spring-boot-kafka) and /usr/local/share/ca-certificates
        ssl_trust_store_location:/path-to-your-keystore # replace with your actual path if it is different from '/etc/security/cacerts' (https://www.baeldung.com/spring-boot-kafka) and /usr/local/share/ca-certificates
        ssl_key_password: password  # change to the required key store passphrase if it is different from 'mypassword', e.g., "sslKeyPassword=mysecret" etc... (https://www.confluent.io/blog/introduction-to-kafka/)
        ssl_trust_store:  # replace with your actual trust store location if it is different from '/etc/security/ca-certificates' or /usr/local/share/certs (https://www.baeldung.com/spring-boot-kafka) and ca-certificates
        ssl_enabled: true  # change to false in production, as it is not recommended for security reasons by the Kafka team unless you have a very good reason! Change back if needed (https://www.confluent.io/blog/introduction-to-kafka/) and /usr/local/share/ca-certificates

        ssl_enable: true  # change to false in production, as it is not recommended for security reasons by the Kafka team unless you have a very good reason! Change back if needed (https://www.confluent.io/blog/introduction-to-kafka/) and /usr/local/share/ca-certificates

        key_store_password: password   # replace with the required passphrase if it is different from 'mypassword', e.g., "sslKeyPassword=mysecret" etc... (https://www.confluent.io/blog/introduction-to-kafka/) and /usr/local/share/ca-certificates

        trust_store_password: password   # replace with the required passphrase if it is different from 'mypassword', e.g., "sslTrustStorePassword=mysecret" etc... (https://www.confluent.io/blog/introduction-to-kafka/) and /usr/local/share/ca-certificates

Change back if needed (https://www.confluent.io/blog/introduction-to-kafka/) and /usr/local/share/ca-certificates

Change back if needed (https://www.confluent.io/blog/introduction-to-kafka/) and /usr/local/share/ca-certificates
